decisions.briefing: Markets were dead flat today — both NASDAQ and S&P 500 closed unchanged — signaling a consolidation session with no major catalysts driving direction. Cash sits at $477, just below the $500 buy-trigger threshold, so no new positions were initiated. All six existing holdings remain intact; no stop-loss or profit-target levels appear to have been breached.

market_education.summary_en: Today's session saw virtually no movement in either major index, with the S&P 500 and NASDAQ both closing flat — a rare 'zero-day' that typically reflects low conviction from both bulls and bears. In the absence of major economic data releases, Fed commentary, or significant corporate news, market participants largely sat on their hands. Such sideways action often occurs mid-week when traders await a catalyst (earnings, macro data, or geopolitical developments) to break the stalemate.
market_education.summary_zh: 今日美股市場幾乎紋風不動，標普500與納斯達克指數雙雙收平，呈現罕見的「零波動日」，反映多空雙方均缺乏明確信念。市場在無重大經濟數據、聯儲局官員發言或企業重大消息的情況下，投資者普遍觀望。此類橫向整固行情通常出現於週中，市場正等待盈利公告、宏觀數據或地緣政治發展等催化劑打破僵局。

daily_lesson.explanation_en: Consolidation is a period when a market or stock moves sideways with little net change, reflecting a temporary balance between buyers and sellers. It often follows a strong move up or down, and represents the market 'digesting' recent gains or losses before committing to the next direction. Traders watch consolidation phases closely because they frequently resolve into a breakout — either continuing the prior trend or reversing it.
daily_lesson.explanation_zh: 整固（Consolidation）是指市場或個股在缺乏方向性動力時橫向盤整的階段，反映買賣雙方暫時達到均衡。整固通常出現在一段強勁漲跌之後，市場正在「消化」近期的升幅或跌幅，為下一步走向蓄積能量。交易者對整固階段格外關注，因為整固往往以突破告終——不論是延續原有趨勢，還是出現趨勢反轉。
